conceptClusters.title: Retrieved paths values and purpose
exitEvidence: Learner resolves image and license references from exact document bases, predicts specification-defined attribute value behavior, and preserves purposeful linked-image navigation.
Learner repairs moved resources, invalid enumerated or numeric values, and ambiguous functional-image links before adding image-specific complexity.

conceptClusters.title: Replaced content and intrinsic evidence
exitEvidence: Learner distinguishes document children from externally replaced content and identifies page-controlled element, box, attributes, resource request, intrinsic data, and external-content boundaries.
Learner traces missing, slow, dimensionless, mismatched, and cross-origin cases through source, request, decode, intrinsic size, layout, paint, and fallback evidence.

conceptClusters.title: Image purpose and alternatives
exitEvidence: Learner classifies informative, functional, decorative, complex, grouped, and text-image purposes from task context rather than visual appearance.
Learner verifies visible, missing-resource, links-only, and accessibility-tree outcomes without redundant or absent task information.

conceptClusters.title: Dimensions loading and failure
exitEvidence: Learner separates intrinsic pixel dimensions, width and height aspect-ratio hints, rendered CSS dimensions, file bytes, loading behavior, decode, and priority evidence.
Learner preserves non-distorted stable layout and meaningful fallback across changed aspect ratios, slow loads, missing resources, and responsive rendered sizes.

conceptClusters.title: Figure and caption relationships
exitEvidence: Learner chooses figure only for self-contained content whose caption forms a meaningful relationship and keeps ordinary-flow images ordinary when clearer.
Learner distinguishes caption, text alternative, surrounding explanation, source credit, and long-form complex-image equivalent in DOM and accessibility evidence.

conceptClusters.title: Rights provenance and permission
exitEvidence: Learner records stable source, creator or owner, permission basis, exact license and version, attribution, modification and commercial-use conditions, evidence date, and uncertainty.
Learner refuses, replaces, or escalates missing, incompatible, disputed, and fair-use-only assets instead of inventing permission or legal certainty.

conceptClusters.title: SVG embedding and meaning
exitEvidence: Learner chooses inline SVG, external image, or decorative treatment using styling, caching, interaction, security, resource, and accessibility constraints.
Learner exposes meaningful graphics and excludes decorative graphics consistently in source, rendered output, missing-resource behavior, and accessibility-tree evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked complete document gains one licensed informative image, stable dimensions, purpose-specific alternative, justified figure relationship, and purpose-matched SVG with synchronized source, network, layout, accessibility, and rights evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A complete document, local image and SVG fixtures, deterministic slow and missing responses, purpose cards, dimension inspector, accessibility tree, and rights ledger are synchronized.
activityDesigns.completionState: The learner predicts and explains twelve contrasts without treating images as ordinary child content, alt as a literal description, dimensions as fixed CSS size, captions as alternatives, search results as permission, or paths as accessible names.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback contrasts the prediction with parsed values, resolved requests, intrinsic data, layout shifts, rendered output, missing-resource fallback, accessibility tree, figure relationships, and exact rights evidence.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance reveals one evidence layer after prediction and keeps technical, accessibility, performance, and permission conclusions separate.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss replays a smaller changed image-purpose or resource case and requires the learner to identify the earliest wrong boundary before continuing.

interactions.title: Resolve image resource references
interactions.learnerAction: Predict resolved image, SVG, credit, and license URLs from nested document bases before inspecting request and target evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Four resource predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Every source value follows the correct document or public base and reaches the intended fixture.
evidence.changedCase: The document and image move in opposite directory directions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Project root or displayed file name becomes the base.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve from each exact referencing document.

interactions.title: Predict image attribute contracts
interactions.learnerAction: Predict parsed and behavioral outcomes for changed src, alt, width, height, loading, decoding, and linked-image href values by value type.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven attribute predictions are recorded.

evidence.observable: URL, free-text, nonnegative-integer, enumerated, and missing-value contracts remain distinct.
evidence.changedCase: Values become absent, invalid, zero, reordered, and differently quoted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every attribute is treated as string truthiness.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify each specification-defined value type.

interactions.title: Inspect linked-image purpose
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a functional image link using href, image purpose, alternative text, link-list name, destination, consequence, and fragment-target evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A linked-image purpose trace is saved.

evidence.observable: The functional alternative names the link action or destination without duplicating adjacent text.
evidence.changedCase: The same graphic changes from decoration to a link with a new destination.

interactions.feedbackTarget: File name or visual motif becomes the link name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the function in links-only context.

interactions.title: Classify replaced-content boundaries
interactions.learnerAction: Classify image, video, iframe, input, inline SVG, and ordinary container cases by document children, replaced resource, intrinsic data, fallback, and page-controlled boundary.
interactions.artifactChange: Six boundary predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Every case identifies the controlled element and box separately from external or intrinsic content.
evidence.changedCase: External image becomes inline SVG and iframe becomes ordinary container.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All visible descendants are treated as child HTML.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect DOM ownership and resource origin.

interactions.title: Trace image load and failure
interactions.learnerAction: Explain source parsing, URL resolution, request, response, bytes, decode, intrinsic dimensions, element box, layout, paint, fallback, and inaccessible external-content boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete replaced-image pipeline is annotated.

evidence.observable: Each stage exposes a distinct page-controlled or resource-controlled input and failure signal.
evidence.changedCase: The response is slow, then missing, then a mismatched media type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS or alt is claimed to modify external pixels.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace ownership and pipeline stage separately.

interactions.title: Classify image purpose
interactions.learnerAction: Classify the same visual asset as informative, functional, decorative, complex, grouped, and text-bearing under six different task contexts.
interactions.artifactChange: Six purpose predictions are locked.

evidence.observable: Every classification follows task information and surrounding equivalence rather than appearance.
evidence.changedCase: Context changes while pixels remain identical.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One literal description is reused everywhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask what task information or function changes.

interactions.title: Inspect alternative outcomes
interactions.learnerAction: Compare meaningful alt, null alt, missing alt, redundant caption text, functional naming, and long-form complex equivalence in rendered and accessibility evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A six-case alternative evidence matrix is saved.

evidence.observable: Visible fallback and accessibility output preserve task information without redundant announcements.
evidence.changedCase: Image purpose and nearby equivalent text both change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Empty and missing alt are treated as equivalent.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect purpose, fallback, and accessibility tree.

interactions.title: Predict dimension evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict intrinsic pixels, width-height aspect-ratio hint, rendered CSS size, distortion, reserved space, and layout movement for six image cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Six dimension predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Intrinsic, attributed, rendered, aspect-ratio, and layout-shift evidence remain distinct.
evidence.changedCase: Source aspect ratio and rendered container width change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attributes are called fixed visual CSS dimensions.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate intrinsic ratio hint from rendered size.

interactions.title: Inspect loading decisions
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect eager, lazy, near-viewport, offscreen, slow, missing, decoded, and undisplayed cases using request timing, reserved geometry, bytes, decode, and paint evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: An eight-case loading timeline is annotated.

evidence.observable: Loading choice, request timing, bytes, decode, geometry, paint, and failure stay causally separate.
evidence.changedCase: An offscreen image becomes the primary above-the-fold content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Lazy is treated as universal performance improvement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match loading decision to position and evidence.

interactions.title: Predict figure relationships
interactions.learnerAction: Choose ordinary flow or figure for six self-contained and context-dependent image cases and distinguish caption, alternative, surrounding explanation, and credit roles.
interactions.artifactChange: Six figure decisions are committed.

evidence.observable: DOM relationships and accessibility evidence match self-contained content and non-redundant roles.
evidence.changedCase: An image becomes independently referenced and gains a data explanation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every image gets figure and caption automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test self-contained relationship and role boundaries.

interactions.title: Inspect media rights evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Compare owned, permission-granted, CC BY, CC BY-SA, CC BY-ND, CC BY-NC, CC0, missing-license, disputed-owner, and fair-use-claim records.
interactions.artifactChange: Ten asset decisions are entered in a rights ledger.

evidence.observable: Each decision records source, owner, permission basis, exact terms, attribution, adaptation, commercial conditions, date, and uncertainty.
evidence.changedCase: The intended adaptation and commercial context change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Credit, search discovery, or education is treated as permission.
interactions.correctionRoute: Require exact authority and conditions or refuse.

interactions.title: Choose SVG embedding semantics
interactions.learnerAction: Explain inline, external-image, linked-functional, and decorative SVG choices using resource, styling, caching, interaction, security, fallback, naming, and accessibility-tree evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A four-case SVG decision map completes the model.

evidence.observable: Meaningful graphics expose the intended name while decorative graphics contribute no redundant task information.
evidence.changedCase: The same graphic changes from decorative external image to interactive inline meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Path data or file name is treated as accessible meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Choose embedding from purpose and boundary evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A museum artifact catalog gains purpose-classified owned or licensed images and SVGs with portable resources, stable loading, meaningful alternatives, justified figures, working functional links, and complete rights evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: A complete museum document set, owned and CC-licensed fixture assets, source manifests, deterministic load failures, artifact fact packet, purpose cards, and empty rights ledger are supplied.
activityDesigns.completionState: The saved and reloaded catalog passes path, value, link-purpose, replaced-boundary, alternative, dimension, loading, figure, rights, SVG, failure, and changed-case checks.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the failed source, request, intrinsic, layout, purpose, accessibility, relationship, permission, or SVG observable without writing final learner code or legal conclusions.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Guided hints move from evidence layer to a smaller parallel artifact and only then to source location or syntax.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss requires prediction, minimal repair, observable evidence, and a changed museum case before the original checkpoint reopens.

interactions.title: Resolve catalog resources
interactions.learnerAction: Predict local image, nested SVG, credit page, creator page, and exact license URLs from each museum document base.
interactions.artifactChange: Five resource predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Every source resolves from its exact document or public base to the intended record.
evidence.changedCase: The catalog page and shared image move in opposite directions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Project root or asset folder is used as universal base.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve each reference from its document URL.

interactions.title: Classify image attribute values
interactions.learnerAction: Classify src, alt, width, height, loading, decoding, href, id, and class values by contract before predicting invalid and missing cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Nine value contracts are annotated.

evidence.observable: URL, text, integer, enumerated, identifier, and token-list behavior remain distinct.
evidence.changedCase: Values become empty, missing, invalid, duplicated, and reordered.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every value is interpreted as generic text or truthiness.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read each attribute's defined microsyntax.

interactions.title: Inspect functional artifact link
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect a linked thumbnail using destination, consequence, image purpose, alt, links-only name, surrounding context, and fragment target evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A functional-image link audit is saved.

evidence.observable: The link name communicates the intended artifact detail destination without redundant visual description.
evidence.changedCase: The thumbnail stops linking and becomes informative content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: File name or artifact appearance becomes link purpose.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name destination or action in links-only output.

interactions.title: Author portable source attributes
interactions.learnerAction: Add valid quoted src and href values for artifact images, SVGs, credits, and licenses while preserving exact owner and value-type contracts.
interactions.artifactChange: Catalog resources gain portable parsed references.

evidence.observable: Parsed values, resolved URLs, request targets, and public records agree.
evidence.changedCase: A credit URL gains encoded query fields and the local resource nests.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source strings pass without request or target evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace parsed value through resolution and response.

interactions.title: Test moved museum resources
interactions.learnerAction: Move an image and credit record, change a public license URL, and repair all source, link-purpose, and provenance references without machine paths.
interactions.artifactChange: Relocated resources regain complete behavior.

evidence.observable: All affected references update, unaffected ones stay stable, and old identities disappear.
evidence.changedCase: Move direction reverses and the destination fragment changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Only visible image src is repaired.
interactions.correctionRoute: Search every old identity and consumer.

interactions.title: Explain resource foundations
interactions.learnerAction: Explain document bases, parsed value types, resource URLs, functional image link purpose, source records, and changed relocation evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A prerequisite evidence map is saved.

evidence.observable: Each reference and purpose claim maps to a distinct parser, resolver, request, or links-only observable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source text and behavior are conflated.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name transformation and consumer per claim.

interactions.title: Predict content ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Predict document children, external resource content, intrinsic data, fallback, and style boundary for image, iframe, video, inline SVG, input, and div cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Six ownership predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Every case distinguishes DOM ownership, element box, replaced data, and controllable boundary.
evidence.changedCase: External SVG becomes inline and ordinary content becomes an image.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All rendered content is treated as DOM descendants.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect nodes, request, and resource ownership.

interactions.title: Inspect artifact image pipeline
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect parsed img, resolved src, request, response, bytes, decode, intrinsic width and height, box, layout, paint, and fallback.
interactions.artifactChange: A complete artifact-image trace is annotated.

evidence.observable: Every pipeline stage and ownership boundary has direct evidence.
evidence.changedCase: The response becomes slow, missing, and then wrong media type.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered pixels are sole proof of a successful pipeline.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace source through every controlled stage.

interactions.title: Test external styling boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Change element box styles, object fitting, alt text, iframe attributes, inline SVG styles, and external SVG pixels while recording controllable effects.
interactions.artifactChange: Six boundary mutations are recorded.

evidence.observable: Page-controlled box and inline content changes stay separate from inaccessible external resource internals.
evidence.changedCase: Same-origin fixture becomes cross-origin and inline SVG becomes external.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS on host element is claimed to restyle external internals.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify ownership before styling claims.

interactions.title: Predict intrinsic data changes
interactions.learnerAction: Predict intrinsic size, aspect ratio, fallback, box geometry, and layout effects when artifact files change dimensions, format, orientation, or fail.
interactions.artifactChange: Five intrinsic-data predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Resource metadata and author-provided hints produce distinct geometry effects.
evidence.changedCase: Portrait asset replaces landscape asset at same URL.

interactions.feedbackTarget: File name or CSS width defines intrinsic size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect decoded resource metadata first.

interactions.title: Test missing artifact resources
interactions.learnerAction: Run absent URL, failed response, zero-byte, corrupt decode, missing alt, empty alt, and informative alt cases while recording output and accessibility evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Seven failure fixtures join the catalog tests.

evidence.observable: Network, decode, fallback, visual, and accessibility failures remain causally distinct.
evidence.changedCase: Failure moves from response to decode and purpose changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All missing images receive same diagnosis or fallback.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate earliest failed stage and intended purpose.

interactions.title: Defend replaced-content model
interactions.learnerAction: Explain DOM ownership, external content, resource requests, intrinsic data, page-controlled boxes, fallback, cross-origin boundary, and changed failures.
interactions.artifactChange: A replaced-content defense is stored.

evidence.observable: Every boundary claim maps to source, DOM, network, decoded metadata, geometry, or accessibility evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Replaced element is reduced to a vocabulary definition.
interactions.correctionRoute: Defend one changed resource end to end.

interactions.title: Classify informative images
interactions.learnerAction: Classify six artifact photos and diagrams by information conveyed, nearby equivalent, task need, and appropriate short or long text alternative.
interactions.artifactChange: Six informative-purpose predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Alternatives convey essential task information without narrating irrelevant pixels.
evidence.changedCase: Nearby prose gains and loses equivalent information.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Literal visual inventory replaces contextual equivalence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify information unavailable without image.

interactions.title: Classify decorative images
interactions.learnerAction: Decide whether six borders, flourishes, repeated motifs, spacer graphics, supplemental photos, and branded marks are decorative in their exact contexts.
interactions.artifactChange: Six decorative decisions are committed.

evidence.observable: Null alternatives exclude only images whose removal loses no task information or function.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative motif becomes the sole status indicator.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Small, pretty, or repeated automatically means decorative.
interactions.correctionRoute: Remove image mentally and test information loss.

interactions.title: Classify functional images
interactions.learnerAction: Predict alternatives for linked thumbnails, icon-only downloads, previous-next graphics, print controls, and image-plus-text links from their functions.
interactions.artifactChange: Five functional alternative predictions are saved.

evidence.observable: Computed link or control names communicate function without duplicate adjacent labels.
evidence.changedCase: A thumbnail changes destination and an icon gains visible text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual description replaces action or destination.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name what activation does.

interactions.title: Classify complex artifact diagrams
interactions.learnerAction: Identify essential overview, relationships, data, labels, and long-form equivalent needs for maps, charts, exploded diagrams, and timelines.
interactions.artifactChange: Four complex-image equivalence plans are committed.

evidence.observable: Short identification and complete nearby or linked equivalent preserve all task-relevant information.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative chart becomes evidence for a conservation decision.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Entire data set is forced into alt or omitted.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate concise identity from full equivalent.

interactions.title: Classify images of text and groups
interactions.learnerAction: Decide alternatives for logos, inscriptions, posters, image-text labels, icon groups, and tiled artifact sequences while preferring real text when possible.
interactions.artifactChange: Six text-and-group decisions are recorded.

evidence.observable: Text equivalence and group meaning are complete without repetitive announcements.
evidence.changedCase: An inscription receives a visible transcript and a tile gains independent meaning.

interactions.feedbackTarget: OCR dump or per-tile repetition replaces purposeful equivalence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify exact text and group-level message.

interactions.title: Author informative alternatives
interactions.learnerAction: Write concise artifact alternatives that convey identity and relevant visual evidence while leaving interpretation and caption context outside the alt value.
interactions.artifactChange: Informative museum images gain contextual alternatives.

evidence.observable: Missing-resource and accessibility-tree outputs preserve essential information without redundancy.
evidence.changedCase: Artifact identity and surrounding caption both change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Alt begins with image of or repeats caption verbatim.
interactions.correctionRoute: State only image-contributed information.

interactions.title: Author decorative exclusions
interactions.learnerAction: Use explicit null alternatives for truly decorative museum graphics and remove fake spacer or status images that should not carry meaning.
interactions.artifactChange: Decorative content stops adding redundant announcements.

evidence.observable: Accessibility tree excludes decoration while visible task information remains complete.
evidence.changedCase: One excluded graphic becomes meaningful after nearby text removal.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Missing alt is used instead of deliberate null alternative.
interactions.correctionRoute: Encode explicit purpose and retest context.

interactions.title: Author functional image names
interactions.learnerAction: Provide functional alternatives for artifact links and icon controls while keeping visible labels, destination-specific purpose, and surrounding context coherent.
interactions.artifactChange: Functional images expose useful activation names.

evidence.observable: Links-only and accessibility-tree outputs identify each action and destination without duplicates.
evidence.changedCase: Destination and adjacent visible label change independently.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Graphic appearance or file type becomes function name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name activation consequence directly.

interactions.title: Test alternatives under failure
interactions.learnerAction: Disable images, fail requests, inspect accessibility output, remove nearby text, change purpose, and verify informative, decorative, functional, and complex cases.
interactions.artifactChange: A purpose-by-failure test matrix is stored.

evidence.observable: Equivalent task information survives changed rendering and context without redundant output.
evidence.changedCase: Each image receives a different purpose and failure mode.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual happy path is accepted as alternative proof.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test missing visual and accessibility outcomes.

interactions.title: Defend purpose decisions
interactions.learnerAction: Explain informative, decorative, functional, complex, text-image, and grouped alternatives using task, context, missing-resource, link, and accessibility evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: A purpose-decision defense is saved.

evidence.observable: Every treatment is tied to exact contextual purpose and changed evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rules are based on visual category alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Defend same pixels in two different contexts.

interactions.title: Inspect intrinsic dimensions
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect decoded pixel dimensions, intrinsic aspect ratio, width and height attributes, rendered CSS box, device scale, and displayed pixels for four assets.
interactions.artifactChange: Four dimension records are annotated.

evidence.observable: Intrinsic metadata, ratio hint, rendered size, and raster density stay distinct.
evidence.changedCase: A higher-density file keeps the same intrinsic ratio and rendered box.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Pixel dimensions are equated with CSS size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name unit and stage for every number.

interactions.title: Author intrinsic dimension hints
interactions.learnerAction: Add accurate nonnegative width and height attributes from verified artifact metadata while leaving responsive rendered sizing to the later CSS layer.
interactions.artifactChange: Catalog images reserve correct aspect-ratio space.

evidence.observable: Reserved geometry matches intrinsic ratio and images remain undistorted after load.
evidence.changedCase: Source file is replaced by a cropped asset with new dimensions.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Arbitrary visual size is written into attributes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use decoded intrinsic ratio evidence.

interactions.title: Predict layout movement
interactions.learnerAction: Predict before-load and after-load geometry for missing dimensions, correct dimensions, wrong ratio, fixed CSS width, fluid CSS width, and absent resource cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Six layout predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Reserved space, rendered size, distortion, and movement predictions match measured geometry.
evidence.changedCase: Container width and image ratio change separately.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Width and height are treated as universal fixed size.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace aspect ratio and rendered constraints.

interactions.title: Predict loading priorities
interactions.learnerAction: Choose eager or lazy behavior for primary artifact, nearby thumbnails, deep gallery images, hidden duplicate, and linked download based on position and task.
interactions.artifactChange: Five loading choices are committed.

evidence.observable: Request timing and paint behavior align with task priority and browser boundaries.
evidence.changedCase: A deep image becomes primary content after navigation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Lazy is applied to every image as a slogan.
interactions.correctionRoute: Match choice to user-needed timing.

interactions.title: Author bounded loading behavior
interactions.learnerAction: Set loading and decoding values for catalog images using verified positions and browser-supported contracts without claiming exact scheduling guarantees.
interactions.artifactChange: Catalog loading intent becomes explicit.

evidence.observable: Parsed values, request timing, decode, geometry, paint, and stated limitations agree.
evidence.changedCase: Viewport start and navigation target change image priority.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Attribute presence is treated as guaranteed performance.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect actual timeline and preserve bounds.

interactions.title: Test slow and failed loading
interactions.learnerAction: Throttle deterministic responses, fail selected resources, corrupt dimensions, and record request, decode, reserved box, shift, fallback, and accessibility outcomes.
interactions.artifactChange: A slow-failure regression suite is stored.

evidence.observable: Stable layout and meaningful alternatives survive each resource failure without distortion.
evidence.changedCase: Failure moves from a decorative image to a functional image.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Fast local load hides missing geometry contracts.
interactions.correctionRoute: Use deterministic delay and failure evidence.

interactions.title: Test responsive rendered sizes
interactions.learnerAction: Change container width, text scale, zoom, orientation, and source intrinsic dimensions while verifying non-distorted rendered boxes and reserved ratios.
interactions.artifactChange: Five geometry mutations challenge the catalog.

evidence.observable: Rendered dimensions adapt while intrinsic and attributed evidence remains correctly interpreted.
evidence.changedCase: Portrait and landscape assets exchange containers.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One desktop screenshot proves image behavior.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare computed and intrinsic geometry across cases.

interactions.title: Explain image performance evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Explain file bytes, request timing, decode, intrinsic dimensions, aspect-ratio hint, rendered size, loading choice, layout movement, paint, and limitations.
interactions.artifactChange: A loading-layout causal explanation is saved.

evidence.observable: Every performance claim maps to a measured stage and changed case.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Smaller, lazy, or dimensions are called universally fast.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tie each claim to one measured outcome.

interactions.title: Choose figure or ordinary flow
interactions.learnerAction: Choose figure or ordinary flow for artifact photo, decorative motif, inline icon, independently referenced diagram, grouped objects, and contextual screenshot.
interactions.artifactChange: Six relationship predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Each choice follows self-contained meaning and surrounding document flow.
evidence.changedCase: A contextual image becomes independently referenced later.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every image is wrapped in figure mechanically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test whether content-caption unit is self-contained.

interactions.title: Author artifact figures
interactions.learnerAction: Build valid figure and figcaption relationships for independently referenced artifact images and diagrams while leaving contextual images in ordinary flow.
interactions.artifactChange: Justified museum figures join the catalog.

evidence.observable: DOM parent-child order, caption association, alternatives, and surrounding references pass.
evidence.changedCase: A figure gains multiple related images and one caption.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Caption placement or element presence passes alone.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect relationship and role of each text source.

interactions.title: Separate caption and alternative
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect six cases where caption, alt, surrounding explanation, credit, and long-form data are duplicated, missing, or assigned the wrong role.
interactions.artifactChange: Six text-role diagnoses are annotated.

evidence.observable: Each text source contributes a distinct non-redundant role in DOM and accessibility output.
evidence.changedCase: Caption and surrounding explanation exchange content.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Figcaption is treated as alt replacement or credit container only.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name purpose and consumer of every text source.

interactions.title: Test figure movement and references
interactions.learnerAction: Move a figure between sections, reorder its content, add an in-text reference, remove its image, and verify context, caption, alternative, and fallback behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Five changed relationship cases are tested.

evidence.observable: Self-contained meaning and references survive valid movement while broken relationships are rejected.
evidence.changedCase: A formerly independent figure becomes essential inline evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: DOM validity alone proves contextual appropriateness.
interactions.correctionRoute: Retest meaning in new document context.

interactions.title: Integrate figure credits correctly
interactions.learnerAction: Place visible artifact caption and source-credit information according to content roles while linking exact creator and license records purposefully.
interactions.artifactChange: Figures gain non-confused captions and credits.

evidence.observable: Caption, attribution, source link, license link, and image alternative remain distinct and useful.
evidence.changedCase: Creator and license records change while caption stays stable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Credit is treated as caption or alt.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate artifact meaning from permission evidence.

interactions.title: Defend figure decisions
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why each museum image is ordinary flow or figure and how caption, alternative, explanation, credit, source, and failure evidence differ.
interactions.artifactChange: A figure-relationship defense is saved.

evidence.observable: Every relationship choice maps to content context, DOM, accessibility, and changed-case evidence.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Figure is defended as styling convenience.
interactions.correctionRoute: Defend self-contained meaning and text roles.

interactions.title: Classify permission bases
interactions.learnerAction: Classify owned, commissioned, express permission, public domain, CC0, Creative Commons, all-rights-reserved, unknown, and fair-use-only asset records.
interactions.artifactChange: Nine permission predictions are committed.

evidence.observable: Each asset receives a bounded use, refusal, replacement, or escalation decision with exact evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Ownership is disputed and intended use becomes commercial.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Search availability, credit, or educational use becomes permission.
interactions.correctionRoute: Require authority and use-specific conditions.

interactions.title: Inspect Creative Commons conditions
interactions.learnerAction: Compare BY, SA, NC, ND, license version, CC0, attribution request, adaptation, commercial use, and downstream sharing for supplied assets.
interactions.artifactChange: A license-condition matrix is annotated.

evidence.observable: Each exact license produces correct attribution, adaptation, commercial, and share-alike boundaries.
evidence.changedCase: A crop becomes a colorized adaptation and use context changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All CC licenses are treated as free without conditions.
interactions.correctionRoute: Read exact license and intended use together.

interactions.title: Record asset provenance
interactions.learnerAction: Create rights records with stable source, creator or owner, asset identity, permission basis, exact license and version, evidence date, and archived proof reference.
interactions.artifactChange: Every selected museum asset gains a provenance record.

evidence.observable: Records resolve to the exact asset, authority, terms, and evidence used for decision.
evidence.changedCase: Source page moves and creator identity is corrected.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A generic website homepage or creator name is enough.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tie stable evidence to exact asset and terms.

interactions.title: Author required attribution

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, responsive, performance, legal, privacy, and duplication reviews are not yet complete.

Learner-facing copy, owned and licensed media fixtures, deterministic load traces, rights records, starter documents, canonical answers, executable checks, browser verification, and learner pilots remain unauthored.
